summary: Drop the claude-code AGENTS.md-memory-kind remnant from the SDK provider std-lib and the built-in lock comment — no AGENTS.md kind ships (Claude Code does not read it).

edit.description: Strip the `agents-md.memory` kind framing from the `memoryAgentsMdDefaultContract` region (builtins.ts:645-667): the doc comment presents it as 'the qualified agents-md.memory kind', which is not a shipped Claude Code kind (Claude Code does not read AGENTS.md). Per the ruling, delete the orphaned `memoryAgentsMdDefaultContract` export (no kind adopts it) OR retain it as a de-framed honest-encoding empty clause array — build's call; say which in the commit body.
Drop the `memoryAgentsMdDefaultContract` re-export (line 22) if the export is deleted; keep it if build retains the export.

Reconcile the header comment (lines 14-26) that lists `memoryAgentsMdDefaultContract` among the default contracts and discusses a distinct `agents-md.memory` kind row — to the seven-kind reality after the drop. The `[declaration.*]` rows are unchanged (guidance-only contract contributed no clause row), so `tests/builtin_lock_frozen.rs` stays green.
Remove the `memoryAgentsMdDefaultContract` import (lines 20,36) and the `is guidance-only — zero clauses` test (lines 118-119) if the export is deleted; keep both if build retains the export.

tests.asserts: The provider suite passes after the drop; no `agents-md.memory` kind framing survives (the guidance-only test is removed with the export, or retained de-framed per build's call).

acceptance: No `agents-md.memory` kind framing survives in `@dtmd/temper/claude-code` or the built-in lock comment; the provider exports the seven shipped kinds' constructs (plus, at build's discretion, a de-framed empty `memoryAgentsMdDefaultContract`); `pnpm --dir sdk test` and `cargo test builtin_lock_frozen` stay green.
notes: Routes inbox `(agents-md-builtin-kind)` (07-15): no claude-code AGENTS.md kind — Claude Code does not read AGENTS.md (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, 2026-07-15). The cross-tool AGENTS.md story now rides `(multi-harness-projection)`. Lock declaration rows unchanged (guidance-only contract = zero clause rows); only the SDK framing + lock header comment move. Build decides delete vs de-framed keep for the export. scoped at 39a4833.

summary: Layout reader reads an unfilled declared region as empty — a prose-only document under a prose+field+collection layout reads green with zero members, not a loud MissingSection.

edit.description: `Layout::read` (kind.rs:340-407) consumes headings purely positionally via `next_heading`, which errors `LayoutError::MissingSection` (enum kind.rs:283, raised 422) when the document runs out of headings for a declared field/collection region. Per the ruling a region 'states what may appear, never what must': read an unfilled region as empty (zero collection members / absent field) rather than erroring. An unadmitted heading fitting no region still refuses via `LayoutError::Unadmitted` (kind.rs:401). NB the reader is positional today; a mid-sequence empty region (headings present for a later region) needs region-to-heading identity matching (slug == slot/member_kind) — build owns the matching mechanism, and surfaces an open question if the ruling underspecifies a case beyond the prose-only acceptance.

Rework `a_document_missing_a_declared_section_fails_loud_naming_file_and_heading` (126-141): it asserts the now-wrong behavior (empty collection => MissingSection). Replace with the ruling's acceptance — a prose-only / trailing-empty-region document reads green with zero members. Keep `an_unadmitted_top_level_heading_refuses_loud` (144-158) unchanged: unadmitted structure still refuses.

tests.asserts: A prose-only document under a layout declaring field + collection regions reads green with those regions empty (zero collection members); a fully-membered document still reads every region; an unadmitted top-level heading still refuses loud.

acceptance: A prose-only (or trailing-region-empty) document under a layout declaring field/collection regions reads green with those regions empty; unadmitted structure still refuses loud; `cargo test layout_kind` green.
notes: Routes inbox `(layout-kind-heterogeneous-corpus)` (07-15) + representation.md amendment (39a4833): regions state what may appear, never what must. Verified on disk — the reader does NOT tolerate empty regions today: layout_kind.rs:126 asserts MissingSection, contradicting the ruling. Floors (required section, min member count) become dialable clauses over the selection — out of scope here, a future entry when demand arrives. scoped at 39a4833.

summary: Flat file kind with a multi-segment or multi-star governs_glob refuses loudly, instead of name-splicing the first `*` and leaving a literal `*` in the derived projection path.

edit.description: `member_projection_path` (drift.rs:555-572) falls to `governs_glob.replacen('*', name, 1)` for any non-directory, non-`**` glob — so a multi-segment or multi-star flat-file glob leaves stray literal `*`/segments in the derived path (silent nonsense today, invariant 6 'Loud or nothing'). Refuse loudly at this derivation (or its emit-time caller) when a flat `file` kind's glob is not a single-segment single-`*` pattern (any-depth `**` still lands the root `<name>.md`); the finding names the two sanctioned shapes for depth — a skill (agent-loaded), a nesting kind (governed content). Any new error variant lives in drift.rs's existing error enum.

Add coverage: a harness declaring a flat `file` kind whose `governs_glob` is multi-segment/multi-star refuses at emit with a finding naming the offending kind and the skill/nesting-kind shapes; a normal single-`*` glob (`*.md`) and an any-depth `**/CLAUDE.md` glob still derive their projection paths unchanged.

tests.asserts: Emitting a harness with a flat file kind whose governs_glob can't map to one projection path fails loud (finding names the kind + the skill/nesting-kind shapes); single-`*` and any-depth `**` globs project unchanged.

acceptance: Emitting a harness with a flat file kind whose `governs_glob` can't map to one projection path fails loud, the finding naming the file and the two sanctioned depth shapes; single-`*` and any-depth `**` globs project unchanged; `cargo test emit` green.
notes: Routes inbox `(directory-sliced-governance)` (07-15): a multi-segment glob on a flat file kind is a loud refusal, never a derived path with a literal `*`. The two sanctioned shapes for depth — a skill (agent-loaded), a nesting kind (governed content) — are the finding's guidance, built when demand arrives, not this entry. `member_projection_path` drifted 513->555 since the note's sha. scoped at 39a4833.

summary: Two kinds declaring the same governs glob refuse loud at check — instead of silently double-routing every matching document into both kinds' member sets.

edit.description: The check assembly (main.rs:717-731) partitions kind rows and emits `kind_collision_diagnostic` (KIND_COLLISION_RULE `kind.admissibility`, main.rs:1414/1469) only for a custom row colliding with a built-in by NAME — nothing checks two kinds sharing a `governs` (root+glob). Add a governs-glob-collision rule over the EFFECTIVE kind set (built-in defs + custom rows, AFTER `row_relocates_builtin` relocation resolution so a legitimate relocation of a built-in's locus is not flagged): when two distinct kinds resolve to the same `governs`, emit an error diagnostic naming both kinds and the shared glob. representation.md: 'two kinds never share a governs glob, because routing a document by its content would be mining.' Strict reading = identical governs (root+glob); build owns whether emit refuses at parity.

Beside the sibling name-collision test `a_kind_row_relocating_a_built_ins_..._fires_no_collision_diagnostic`, add coverage: two custom kinds declaring an identical `governs` fire the glob-collision diagnostic naming both kinds + the glob; disjoint governs (and the shipped built-in set) fire nothing; a legitimate same-shape built-in relocation still fires no collision. Build may prefer a focused test file — name the choice in the commit body.

tests.asserts: Two kinds with an identical governs glob fire an error collision diagnostic naming both kinds + the glob; disjoint globs and the built-in set fire nothing; a same-shape built-in relocation is not a collision.

acceptance: Two kinds sharing a governs glob refuse loud at check, the finding naming both kinds and the shared glob; the shipped built-in kind set (disjoint globs) and a legitimate relocation fire nothing; `cargo test` green.
notes: Routes 39a4833 representation.md: 'two kinds never share a governs glob — position types, content never routes.' Sibling of the NAME-collision rule (KIND_COLLISION_RULE, main.rs:1414), which does NOT cover governs. Verified no glob-collision check exists; per-kind discovery (import.rs) double-routes silently today. Overlapping-but-distinct globs (`*.md` vs `foo.md`) are a harder intersection question, out of scope — flag if build hits it. scoped at be3bd27.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
